mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding real economic activity, increasing corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between INDPRO changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent CPI inflation erodes real purchasing power and compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, leading to negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The mean 60-day SPX return following CPIAUCSL increases in the top tertile is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.25%, it marks the start of a tightening cycle that increases the cost of capital and reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative returns over the following month.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25%, the average 20-day SPX return is positive or not significantly different from zero (Wilcoxon signed-rank test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE drops below 4%, the labor market is overheated, leading to wage-driven inflation and anticipated Fed tightening, which depresses equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: The median 5-day SPX return after UNRATE drops below 4% is greater than or equal to zero (sign test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI and the federal funds rate indicate the Fed is hiking to combat entrenched inflation, raising real rates and sharply reducing equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise month-over-month, the average 20-day SPX return is positive or zero (one-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with rising industrial production reflect robust domestic demand and supply-side capacity, boosting corporate profits and pushing S&P 500 higher over the next week.
failure_condition: The rank IC of the interaction term (RSAFS growth * INDPRO growth) for 5-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rise strongly when interest rates are low, signaling a booming construction sector and positive economic multiplier effects that lift equity markets over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS below 2%, the correlation between HOUST growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (Spearman rho < 0.10, p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains in the early expansion phase of the business cycle boost consumer income and spending, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 5% and declining, the average 20-day SPX return following strong PAYEMS growth (top quartile) is negative or not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
